hypertension.key_factor: Zero sodium, zero sugar, zero calories
hypertension.guidance: Flavoured oolong tea (no sugar) contains 0 mg sodium and 0 g sugar per 100 ml, making it fully compliant with DASH dietary guidelines for hypertension management; patients should verify the product label confirms no added sweeteners or sodium-containing additives.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Zero carbohydrate, zero sugar, negligible glycemic impact
type2_diabetes.guidance: With 0 g carbohydrate and 0 g sugar per 100 ml, this beverage produces no postprandial glycemic response and is appropriate for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes; oolong tea polyphenols have been associated with improved insulin sensitivity in peer-reviewed research, though patients should confirm no hidden sugars or non-nutritive sweeteners are present in the specific product formulation.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Zero saturated fat, zero sodium, zero sugar; tea polyphenol content
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Flavoured oolong tea (no sugar) contains no saturated fat, no sodium, and no added sugar, aligning with AHA dietary recommendations for cardiovascular disease risk reduction; regular consumption of unsweetened oolong tea has been associated with modest improvements in LDL cholesterol and endothelial function in observational studies, though packaged flavoured variants may have lower polyphenol concentrations than freshly brewed tea.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Polyphenol content (theaflavins, catechins); zero pro-inflammatory nutrients
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Oolong tea polyphenols including theaflavins and catechins have documented anti-inflammatory properties relevant to rheumatoid arthritis management; the absence of sugar and sodium removes pro-inflammatory dietary triggers, though patients should confirm the specific product contains no added sweeteners.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Flavoured Oolong Tea (No Sugar) is a ready-to-drink packaged beverage made from brewed oolong tea with added natural or artificial flavourings and no added sugar. Oolong tea is a traditional Chinese tea that is partially oxidised, placing it between green tea and black tea in terms of oxidation level and flavour profile. The beverage has a light, floral to slightly roasted taste depending on the flavouring used, with a smooth, mildly astringent finish. In Singapore and Southeast Asia, flavoured oolong tea is widely available in bottles and cans as a convenient zero-calorie drink option. Nutritionally, this product contains 0 kcal, 0 g protein, 0 g fat, and 0 g carbohydrate per 100 ml, making it essentially calorie-free and suitable for calorie-restricted, low-carbohydrate, and ketogenic dietary patterns. The absence of sugar and sodium makes it appropriate for individuals managing hypertension, Type 2 Diabetes, and cardiovascular disease. Oolong tea naturally contains polyphenols such as theaflavins and catechins, which have been associated with anti-inflammatory effects, improved insulin sensitivity, and modest cardiovascular benefits in peer-reviewed research. Caffeine is naturally present in oolong tea at approximately 30–50 mg per 250 ml serving, which patients with caffeine sensitivity should consider. The glycemic index is not applicable as the beverage contains negligible carbohydrates.

notes: Zero-macro justification: protein_g is confirmed zero — flavoured oolong tea (no sugar) is an aqueous tea beverage containing no protein-bearing ingredients. fat_g is confirmed zero by product formulation; no fat-containing ingredients are present. carbohydrate_g is confirmed zero per HPB Singapore Foodo for the no-sugar variant; USDA brewed tea data shows 0.3 g/100 ml for plain brewed tea, but the HPB-reported zero is accepted for the packaged no-sugar product formulation. energy_kcal is confirmed zero consistent with zero macronutrients. Saturated fat, monounsaturated fat, polyunsaturated fat, and trans fat are all confirmed zero by formulation as no fat is present. sugar_g is confirmed zero — this is the defining characteristic of the no-sugar product variant. dietary_fibre_g is confirmed zero as tea solids are not present in meaningful quantities in the liquid beverage. sodium_mg is confirmed zero per HPB. calcium_mg and iron_mg are confirmed zero per HPB. cholesterol_mg is confirmed zero as no animal-derived ingredients are present. vitamin_b12_mcg and vitamin_d_mcg are confirmed zero as this is a plant-derived beverage with no fortification reported. water_g estimated at 99.7 g/100 ml consistent with USDA brewed tea data. Negative sentinel values: vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg set to -1: detailed micronutrient data for packaged flavoured oolong tea (no sugar) is not available in HPB Singapore Foodo or USDA FoodData Central as of 2026. Glycemic index field is set to numeric 0 per template numeric requirement; gi_category is set to not_applicable to indicate GI measurement is not meaningful for this zero-carbohydrate beverage. Caffeine content is not captured in the per_100g schema but is naturally present in oolong tea at approximately 30–50 mg per 250 ml serving. Ontology codes are approximate matches; no exact FoodOn or FoodEx2 entry exists for flavoured oolong tea (no sugar) as a distinct product category.
